content.claim: The church-maintained schools sector is positioned as a distinct veto constituency; a figure close to the Tisza government publicly stated it is in 'everyone's common interest' to dispel assumptions that church schools or 6/8-year schools would be abolished, indicating the government perceives this stakeholder bloc as requiring explicit reassurance to prevent mobilization against reform.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Attribution is indirect ('a figure close to the Tisza government') and does not reflect official government position
No evidence provided on the magnitude of church-school enrollment in 6/8-year tracks or actual veto capacity
Statement may reflect precautionary communication rather than evidence of genuine veto threat
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
